title: Introducing Bike School - New Economic/Educational Model for America & the World

enrichment.summary: Kyle introduces 'Bike School,' his concept for a new educational model where students learn by riding bikes alongside a peer-teacher mentor while listening to audiobooks, replacing traditional high school and college with a 20-year program rooted in history. He contrasts this with the traditional school system's bureaucratic inefficiency and argues that an education grounded in history produces informed, politically capable citizens. He also introduces his 'Sanctum Onim' fantasy-overlay concept as a way to make historical geography tangible during rides.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: bloated with moving parts — costly, coercive, and produces passive followers rather than educated citizens
a self-perpetuating, low-overhead model that aligns learning with what people already want to do
should be peer-based like ancient Spartan mentorship — both learning together — not a top-down lecture dynamic
history is the backbone of all subjects and the best way to produce politically literate citizens
fails because it treats students as passive, strips choice, and produces people easily manipulated by media and politicians
people naturally want to learn; the goal is a framework that channels that motivation rather than coercing compliance
should replace high school and college — a 20-year program from age 13 to 33
ancient societies had social structures refined over thousands of years that we should model rather than discard
